claim: MCP (Model Context Protocol) is a universal standard that eliminates the need for custom integrations between AI apps and external tools—like USB for AI
actionable: Use MCP servers to give agents access to external tools and data without custom code for each integration

claim: MCP servers expose three components: tools (callable functions), resources (read-only data), and prompt templates (structured prompts for better outputs)
actionable: When building MCP servers, include tools for actions, resources for static data, and prompt templates to guide users

claim: MCP client-server architecture uses two transport types: local (stdio) for same-machine communication and HTTP-SSE/streamable for remote servers
actionable: Choose transport based on deployment: stdio for local agents, HTTP with SSE (stateful) or streamable HTTP (stateless) for remote

claim: Build no-code MCP servers in N8N by connecting tools (Gmail, calculator, etc.) and exposing them via a production URL
actionable: Use N8N workflow builder to define tools, get production URL, then connect to host via MCP client config

claim: In code-based MCP servers, decorate Python functions with @mcp.tool() to expose them as tools to the LLM
actionable: Define Python functions with docstrings, decorate with @mcp.tool(), and strands/Anthropic SDKs automatically expose to agents

claim: Prompt templates in MCP servers offload prompt engineering burden from users by providing pre-optimized instructions for specific workflows
actionable: Include hardcoded prompt templates in MCP servers for common tasks (e.g., analyze sheet data, create report) so users get better results

claim: Over 20,000 pre-built MCP servers are now available for common tasks (data access, CRM, version control), massively reducing integration friction
actionable: Before building custom integrations, search the MCP registry for pre-built servers that already solve your problem

claim: MCP client configuration (for hosts like Claude Desktop) is done via JSON config in .claude/claude_desktop_config.json, specifying server name and endpoint
actionable: Configure MCP servers in host config by specifying transport type, endpoint, and credentials

claim: MCP standardization means agents can switch hosts (Claude to N8N to custom app) while keeping the same servers without re-writing integrations
actionable: Design agent systems to use MCP servers; this preserves flexibility to change hosts later
